title: Smt. Bismillah Bi Neelgar vs Ramlal on 23 October, 2024
body: Smt. Bismillah Bi Neelgar vs Ramlal on 23 October, 2024 NEUTRAL CITATION NO. 2024:MPHC-IND:30911 1 MP-2266-2021 IN THE HIGH COURT OF MADHYA PRADESH AT INDORE BEFORE HON'BLE SHRI JUSTICE GAJENDRA SINGH ON THE 23rd OF OCTOBER, 2024 MISC. PETITION No. 2266 of 2021 SMT. BISMILLAH BI NEELGAR Versus RAMLAL AND OTHERS Appearance: Mr. Manish Manana - learned counsel for the petitioner. Ms. Anita Singh, learned PL for the respondent no.5/State. ORDER This Misc. Petition under Article 227 of the Constitution of India is preferred being aggrieved by the order dated 11.08.2024 in Revision Case No.20/Nigrani/2019-20 by the Collector, Ratlam arising out of the order dated 03.01.2020 in Case No.56/Appeal/2019-20 by the Sub-Divisional Officer, Jaora and order dated 23.07.2019 in Case No.13/A-13/2016-2017 by the Tehsildar Jaora. 02. Facts of the case in brief are that the respondents no.1 to 4 filed the application under Section 131 of the Madhya Pradesh Land Revenue Code before the Tehsildar, Jaora submitting that they are the cultivators of agricultural land of Survey No.412/1, 412/1/1/kha, 412/1/G and have access through the agricultural land of Survey No.412/1, 416/2 through the land of Survey No.345/1101 since long. The land of Survey No.345/1101 was sold by Dilip Singh to Bismillah Bi and Bismillah Bi obstructed their access to Signature Not Verified Signed by: VARSHA SINGH Signing time: 26-10-2024 16:15:34 NEUTRAL CITATION NO. 2024:MPHC-IND:30911 2 MP-2266-2021 above said agricultural land. On the application after inquiry vide order dated 23.07.2019, the Tehsildar, Jaora passed the order to remove the obstruction and open the route. The order of the Tehsildar was challenged before the Sub-Divisional Officer, Jaora but the same was dismissed. Thereafter, both the orders were challenged before the Collector Ratlam by way of Revision and the revision petitioner was again unsuccessful in revision also. 03. Challenging the above orders, this Misc. Petition is preferred on the ground that allegation of obstructing the route way by laying Murram was total false. In reply dated 24.07.2012, it was specifically stated that the approach for the field of Ramlal was near to the cremation ground, then Dadiamma ki Dargah and then to their field. No path was ever existing from the land of the petitioner. The sale deed executed by Dilip Kumar in favour of petitioner Bismillah Bee dated 12.11.2007 carries no whisper of any easementory rights from the land sold. The provisions of Order 6 Rule 17 of the CPC never applies to proceedings U/S 131 of MPLRC. The Tehsildar committed grave error of law in considering a totally new case framed under the garb of amendment. By way of amendment a controversial statement has been made regarding availability of easementoary rights from the land of the Petitioner and learned Tehsildar has not considered that previous application was moved for removal of obstruction and by way of amendment Ramlal and others stated that their approach way is still available from the land of the Petitioner. The controversial application was liable to be dismissed. 04. In paragraph 2 of their amended application Ramlal & others stated that if the approach to their field is obstructed by the petitioners then Signature Not Verified Signed by: VARSHA SINGH Signing time: 26-10-2024 16:15:34 NEUTRAL CITATION NO. 2024:MPHC-IND:30911 3 MP-2266-2021 they will suffer serious problems. The Tehsildar has not considered the fact that before inspection of the spot, the petitioner was not noticed. The inspection was not carried out in the presence of petitioner and in the absence of the petitioner a false memorandum has been prepared. The Map which was considered by the Tehsildar carries no evidence that previously any path was ever available to the Ramlal & others. Exceeding its jurisdiction, the Tehsildar has recorded the statements on affidavit. The procedure of recording examination-in-chief under Order 18 Rule 4 C.P.C . applies to the Civil Suit and not to a proceeding under section 131 of the MPLRC. No opportunity of leading evidence has been provided to the present petitioner. 05. The Revenue Officers erred in not taking into consideration the complaint filed by the Police Station Industrial Area, Jaora under Section 145 of the Cr.P.C. in which it was found after investigation that Ramlal and his brothers were trying to create new path through Road Roller and that too from the centre of the Agricultural Land of the petitioner. The letter from Sub-Divisional Officer addressing to the Chowki Prabhari of Husain Tekri also discloses that the path was never existing from the agricultural of the petitioner and Ramlal is trying to create a new approach to his field. All the Revenue Courts has not at all considered the issue involved between Ramlal and Bishmillah Bi pertains to the title and should be decided by the Civil Courts. Under Section 131 of the MPLRC only obstruction can be removed but new rights can never be created under the garb of Section 131 of the MPLRC. Four boundaries clearly specified that there is spruit on the nothern side of the petitioners land. It is sufficient to nullify the claim of Ramlal & Signature Not Verified Signed by: VARSHA SINGH Signing time: 26-10-2024 16:15:34 NEUTRAL CITATION NO. 2024:MPHC-IND:30911 4 MP-2266-2021 others that on the nothern side of the petitioners land there approach path is existing. 06. None for the respondents. Heard. Perused the record. 07. The respondents no.1 to 4 filed the Annexure-P/5 before the SDO, Jaora in which it is claimed that they have a route to access their fields of Survey No.412/A, 412/1/Kha, 410/2, 412/1/G, 414/1 and 416/2 since long through the neighbouring cultivator Dilip Kumar's land and Nala but Dilip Kumar sold the land to petitioner Bismillah Bi and son and husband of the Bismillah Bi is obstructing their route to approach the field and for this purpose has laid Muram. The order dated 23.07.2019 (Annexure-P/3) discloses that 3 spot inspections were undertaken through different authorities and in all 3 spot inspection the existence of the route through field of Dilip Kumar and now Bismillah Bi was found but the route was obstructed through Muram. 08. The provisions of Section 131 of the MPLRC discloses that the matter has to be decided by a Tehsildar after local inquiry with reference to the previous custom in each case and with due regard to the conveniences of all the parties concerned and for the purpose of local inquiry, the spot inspection have value. 09. The petitioner have referred to Exhibit-P/9 i.e. a letter of SDO (Revenue) addressed In-charge of Police Chowki, Husain Tekri, Jaora in which it is stated that till receiving of Patwari report, the respondent be Signature Not Verified Signed by: VARSHA SINGH Signing time: 26-10-2024 16:15:34 NEUTRAL CITATION NO. 2024:MPHC-IND:30911 5 MP-2266-2021 restrained to use the new road and the same letter has been marked as Annexure-P/10 and P/11 and theses annexures does not indicate that the respondents were creating new way. The contents of Annexure-P/5 are sufficient to demonstrate the nature of a claim under Section 131 of the MPLRC. The sale deed executed in favour of Bismillah Bi cannot be the basis to decide the controversy under Section 131 of the MPLRC. 10. The proceedings of Tehsildar discloses that the order has been passed conducting a local inquiry envisaged under Section 131 of the M.P.L.R.C. and within the scope of Article 227 of the Constitution of India, the order dated 23.07.2019 passed by Tehsildar, Jaora, order dated 03.01.2020 by the Sub-Divisional Officer, Jaora and order dated 11.08.2024 by the Collector, Ratlam does not call for interference. Accordingly, Misc. Petition stands dismissed and disposed of. (GAJENDRA SINGH) JUDGE VS Signature Not Verified Signed by: VARSHA SINGH Signing time: 26-10-2024 16:15:34
